judgmentEn: The Creative works sublime success, furthering through perseverance.
imageZh: 天行健，君子以自强不息。
imageEn: The movement of heaven is full of power. Thus the superior man makes himself strong and untiring.
interpretationZh: 乾卦象征天，代表刚健、创造、进取。六爻皆阳，纯阳至刚，是最具开创力量的卦象。
interpretationEn: Qian represents heaven and pure creative power. All six lines are yang, symbolizing strength, initiative, and the primal creative force.
overviewZh: 乾卦是《周易》六十四卦之首，由六个阳爻组成，纯阳至刚，象征天道运行不息。乾为天，代表宇宙间最原始、最强大的创造力量。此卦以龙为喻，从潜龙到飞龙再到亢龙，描绘了事物从萌芽、发展、鼎盛到衰退的完整生命周期。乾卦的核心精神是“自强不息”——天体运行永不停歇，君子当效法天道，持续精进，刚健有为。乾卦四德“元亨利贞”分别代表万物之始、通达亨通、和谐有利、正固持守，是宇宙运行的根本法则，也是为人处世的最高准则。
overviewEn: Qian (The Creative) is the first hexagram of the I Ching, composed of six unbroken yang lines representing pure creative power. It symbolizes heaven and the ceaseless movement of celestial forces. Using the dragon as its central metaphor, it traces the complete arc of development—from the hidden dragon lying dormant, through the dragon appearing in the field, to the flying dragon in the heavens, and finally the arrogant dragon facing regret. The four virtues of Qian—yuan (sublime), heng (success), li (furthering), and zhen (perseverance)—represent the fundamental principles governing the cosmos and human conduct. The hexagram teaches that true strength lies not in brute force but in tireless self-cultivation and knowing when to advance or retreat.
judgmentDetailZh: 乾卦卦辞“元亨利贞”四字，被称为乾之四德，是整部《周易》最核心的概念之一。“元”为万物之始，代表创始、仁德，是春天万物萌发的力量；“亨”为通达，代表事物顺利发展，如夏天万物繁茂；“利”为和谐适宜，代表秋天收获的正当性；“贞”为正固，代表冬天收藏的坚守。四德循环往复，如同四季更替，生生不息。孔子在《文言传》中将四德与人伦相对应：元为仁，亨为礼，利为义，贞为智。修身之人若能体悟四德，便能与天道合一，成就大业。乾卦告诫我们：真正的刚强不是一味进取，而是在不同阶段采取恰当的行动——该潜则潜，该飞则飞，该止则止。
judgmentDetailEn: The four characters of Qian's judgment—yuan, heng, li, zhen—are known as the Four Virtues of the Creative, forming one of the most essential concepts in the entire I Ching. 'Yuan' (Sublime) represents the origin of all things, the power of spring when life begins to stir. 'Heng' (Success) signifies smooth development, like summer when all things flourish. 'Li' (Furthering) embodies harmony and appropriateness, the rightful harvest of autumn. 'Zhen' (Perseverance) stands for steadfast correctness, the conservation of winter. These four virtues cycle endlessly like the seasons. Confucius, in the Wenyan Commentary, mapped them to human virtues: yuan as benevolence, heng as propriety, li as righteousness, and zhen as wisdom. The hexagram teaches that true strength is not relentless aggression but knowing the right action for each phase—when to lie hidden, when to soar, and when to stop.
imageDetailZh: 象辞“天行健，君子以自强不息”是中华文明最著名的格言之一，出自《大象传》对乾卦的解读。“天行健”描述的是天体运行刚健有力、永不停歇的自然现象——日月星辰各循其道，从不懈怠。“君子以自强不息”则是从天道推及人道：有德行的人应当效法天的精神，持续不断地修养自身、精进学问、完善品格。这里的“自强”不是与他人争强好胜，而是对自我的不断超越；“不息”不是不知疲倦地蛮干，而是持之以恒的坚定意志。清华大学校训“自强不息，厚德载物”即取自乾坤两卦象辞，体现了中华文化刚柔并济的最高理想。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'The movement of heaven is full of power; thus the superior man makes himself strong and untiring' is one of the most celebrated maxims in Chinese civilization. 'The movement of heaven is full of power' describes the ceaseless, vigorous motion of celestial bodies—the sun, moon, and stars each following their course without rest. 'The superior man makes himself strong and untiring' extends this cosmic principle to human conduct: a person of virtue should emulate heaven's spirit through continuous self-cultivation, learning, and character refinement. The 'strength' here is not about competing with others but about transcending one's own limitations. The 'untiring' quality is not reckless exertion but unwavering perseverance. Tsinghua University's motto—'Self-discipline and social commitment'—draws directly from the Image texts of Qian and Kun, embodying the Chinese ideal of balancing firmness with gentleness.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】乾卦提醒我们要像龙一样审时度势：创业初期如“潜龙”，应低调积累实力；事业上升期如“见龙在田”，要积极寻找导师和合作伙伴；事业巅峰如“飞龙在天”，可大展宏图但不可骄傲自满；而“亢龙有悔”则警示功成名就后要懂得急流勇退。【感情】乾卦代表阳刚主动的能量，提示在关系中要真诚、有担当，但也要避免过于强势。【财运】乾卦象征创造力和开拓精神，适合开创新事业、投资新领域，但要注意“亢龙有悔”的风险——贪心不足会导致损失。【健康】乾卦对应头部和肺部，提醒注意用脑过度和呼吸系统保养，保持规律运动，效法天道“自强不息”。
modernApplicationEn: Qian offers profound guidance for modern life. In career, it reminds us to read the situation like the dragon: during early stages ('hidden dragon'), build strength quietly; during growth ('dragon in the field'), actively seek mentors and partners; at the peak ('flying dragon'), pursue grand visions without arrogance; and 'the arrogant dragon' warns that after success, one must know when to step back. In relationships, Qian represents proactive yang energy—be sincere and responsible, but avoid being overbearing. For finances, Qian symbolizes creativity and pioneering spirit, favoring new ventures and investments, while cautioning against the 'arrogant dragon' risk of overreach leading to loss. For health, Qian corresponds to the head and lungs—watch for mental exhaustion and respiratory care, maintain regular exercise, and embody heaven's spirit of tireless self-strengthening.
historicalStoryZh: 乾卦最著名的历史典故当属孔子读《易》“韦编三绝”的故事。相传孔子晚年酷爱研读《周易》，尤其对乾卦反复揣摩，以至于编连竹简的牛皮绳断了三次。孔子从乾卦中悟出“天行健，君子以自强不息”的道理，将其作为毕生修身的准则。另一个典故是诸葛亮以乾卦自勉。诸葛亮隐居隆中时如“潜龙勿用”，韬光养晦等待明主；刘备三顾茅庐后出山辅佐，如“见龙在田”；北伐中原时如“飞龙在天”，鞠躬尽瘁。诸葛亮的一生完美诠释了乾卦六爻从潜到飞的精神境界，成为后世“自强不息”的典范。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous historical story associated with Qian is Confucius and his devoted study of the I Ching. In his later years, Confucius studied the Book of Changes so intensely—particularly the Qian hexagram—that the leather straps binding his bamboo scrolls broke three times (a story known as 'wei bian san jue'). From Qian, he derived the principle of tireless self-improvement that guided his entire life. Another celebrated story involves Zhuge Liang, the legendary strategist of the Three Kingdoms period. While living in seclusion at Longzhong, he embodied the 'hidden dragon'—quietly cultivating his abilities while awaiting the right leader. When Liu Bei visited him three times to seek his counsel, Zhuge Liang emerged like the 'dragon appearing in the field.' His Northern Expeditions represented the 'flying dragon in the heavens,' as he devoted himself completely to his cause. Zhuge Liang's life perfectly illustrates the spiritual journey of Qian's six lines.
relatedHexagramsNote: 乾卦的错卦为坤卦（第2卦），阴阳完全相反，刚柔互补；综卦亦为乾卦本身（上下颠倒不变）；互卦为乾卦（取二三四五爻组成的内卦仍为纯阳）。乾坤两卦互为表里，共同构成《周易》的根基。

lines.textZh: 初九：潜龙勿用。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Hidden dragon. Do not act.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙潜于深渊，时机未到，宜韬光养晦。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon is hidden. It is not yet time to act.
lines.detailZh: 初九为乾卦最下一爻，阳气初生，力量尚弱，如龙潜伏于深水之中。此时万事草创，条件尚未成熟，不宜贸然行动。“潜”字是关键——不是没有能力，而是时机未到。如同种子在土壤中积蓄力量，等待破土而出的那一刻。智者在此阶段应当韬光养晦，默默积累学识、人脉和资源，为将来的腾飞做好充分准备。历史上许多成大事者，都经历过漫长的“潜龙”阶段。
lines.detailEn: The first nine is the lowest line of Qian, where yang energy has just been born and is still weak—like a dragon hidden deep beneath the waters. Conditions are not yet ripe for action. The key word is 'hidden'—not lacking ability, but awaiting the right moment. Like a seed gathering strength underground before breaking through the soil, the wise person in this phase should quietly accumulate knowledge, connections, and resources, preparing thoroughly for future ascent. Many great historical figures endured long periods as the 'hidden dragon' before achieving greatness.

lines.textZh: 九二：见龙在田，利见大人。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Dragon appearing in the field. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙出现在田野，开始崭露头角，宜寻求贵人指引。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon shows itself. Seek guidance from a wise mentor.
lines.detailZh: 九二阳爻居阴位，刚柔相济，龙从深渊浮出水面，出现在广阔的田野上。这是崭露头角的阶段——才华开始被人发现，但尚未达到权力的中心。“利见大人”意味着此时最重要的是寻找良师益友、贵人提携。田野象征广阔的舞台，龙已经具备了施展才华的基本条件。在现代语境中，这相当于职场新人开始展现实力，需要主动寻求导师指导，建立专业声誉，为进一步发展奠定基础。
lines.detailEn: The second nine is a yang line in a yin position, blending firmness with flexibility. The dragon has risen from the depths to appear in the open field—a stage of emerging visibility where talents begin to be recognized, though one has not yet reached the center of power. 'It furthers one to see the great man' means the most important action now is to seek wise mentors and benefactors. The field symbolizes a broad stage where the dragon has the basic conditions to display its abilities. In modern terms, this is like a professional beginning to demonstrate competence—actively seeking mentorship, building reputation, and laying foundations for further advancement.

lines.textZh: 九三：君子终日乾乾，夕惕若厉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: All day long the superior man is creatively active. At nightfall his mind is still beset with cares. Danger. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 白天勤勉努力，夜晚保持警惕，虽有危险但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Be diligent by day and vigilant by night. Danger exists but no blame.
lines.detailZh: 九三处于下卦之顶，上不在天、下不在田，位置尴尬而危险。“终日乾乾”形容白天不停地勤勉努力，“夕惕若厉”则是夜晚仍保持高度警觉，如临深渊。这一爻描绘的是事业上升期最关键也最危险的阶段——已经取得一定成就，但根基尚不稳固，上有强者压制，下有后来者追赶。唯有日夜不懈、谨慎行事，才能化险为夷。“无咎”二字说明：只要保持这种勤勉警惕的态度，即使处境危险也不会有过失。
lines.detailEn: The third nine sits at the top of the lower trigram—neither in heaven nor in the field—an awkward and dangerous position. 'All day long creatively active' describes ceaseless diligence during the day, while 'at nightfall beset with cares' means maintaining acute vigilance at night, as if standing at the edge of an abyss. This line depicts the most critical and perilous phase of career ascent—having achieved some success but with foundations not yet secure, pressed from above and pursued from below. Only through tireless effort and careful conduct can one navigate safely through. The words 'no blame' confirm that maintaining this diligent, vigilant attitude prevents fault even in dangerous circumstances.

lines.textZh: 九四：或跃在渊，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Wavering flight over the depths. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在深渊上方跃动，进退皆可，把握时机则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Hovering between advance and retreat. Seize the right moment.
lines.detailZh: 九四进入上卦，已接近权力核心但尚未到达顶点。“或跃在渊”中的“或”字极为精妙——表示可进可退、可跃可潜的灵活状态。龙在深渊上方盘旋，随时准备一飞冲天，但也可以选择退回深渊继续蛰伏。这是一个充满选择的关键时刻，考验的是判断力和决断力。此爻教导我们：在重大转折点上，不必急于做出决定，审时度势、保持灵活才是上策。无论最终选择进还是退，只要出于审慎的判断，都不会有过失。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine enters the upper trigram, approaching the center of power but not yet at the summit. The character 'huo' (perhaps/or) in 'wavering flight over the depths' is brilliantly nuanced—it suggests the flexibility to advance or retreat, to leap or to remain hidden. The dragon hovers above the abyss, ready to soar skyward at any moment yet equally able to return to the depths. This is a critical juncture full of choices, testing one's judgment and decisiveness. The line teaches that at major turning points, there is no need to rush decisions—assessing the situation and maintaining flexibility is the wisest course. Whether one ultimately advances or retreats, as long as the choice comes from careful deliberation, there will be no blame.

lines.textZh: 九五：飞龙在天，利见大人。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Flying dragon in the heavens. It furthers one to see the great man.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙飞在天，事业达到巅峰，大展宏图之时。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon flies in the heavens. A time of great achievement.
lines.detailZh: 九五是乾卦的核心爻位，阳爻居阳位，又处上卦之中，刚健中正，是最理想的位置。飞龙翱翔于天际，象征事业达到巅峰、才德兼备、众望所归。“利见大人”在此有双重含义：一是自己已成为“大人”，德才足以领导众人；二是仍需与其他贤能之士合作共事。九五之尊在中国文化中成为帝王的代称，因为它代表了权力与德行的完美结合。此爻提醒我们：即使身处巅峰，也要保持谦逊，广纳贤才，以德服人，方能长久。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is the core line of Qian—a yang line in a yang position at the center of the upper trigram, embodying perfect strength and balance. The flying dragon soaring through the heavens symbolizes reaching the pinnacle of achievement with both virtue and ability, commanding universal respect. 'It furthers one to see the great man' carries dual meaning here: one has become the 'great man' with sufficient virtue to lead others, yet one still needs to collaborate with other worthy individuals. In Chinese culture, 'the nine in the fifth place' became synonymous with the emperor, representing the perfect union of power and virtue. This line reminds us that even at the summit, one must remain humble, welcome talented people, and lead through moral authority to ensure lasting success.

lines.textZh: 上九：亢龙有悔。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Arrogant dragon will have cause to repent.
lines.interpretationZh: 龙飞得太高，物极必反，过刚则折，宜知进退。
lines.interpretationEn: The dragon has flown too high. Excess leads to regret.
lines.detailZh: 上九是乾卦最高一爻，阳气已达极盛，物极必反。龙飞得太高，高处不胜寒，已经脱离了与大地的联系。“亢”意为过度、极端，“悔”则是后悔、遗憾。这是整部《周易》最深刻的警示之一：任何事物发展到极端都会走向反面。功成名就后若不知收敛，必将招致灾祸。历史上无数英雄豪杰在巅峰时期因骄傲自满而跌落，正应了“亢龙有悔”的预言。此爻教导我们：知进退、明得失，功成身退才是最高的智慧。月满则亏，水满则溢，这是不可违背的自然法则。
lines.detailEn: The top nine is the highest line of Qian, where yang energy has reached its absolute peak and must inevitably reverse. The dragon has flown too high—isolated at the summit, disconnected from the earth below. 'Kang' means excess or extreme; 'hui' means regret or remorse. This is one of the most profound warnings in the entire I Ching: anything pushed to its extreme will reverse course. Those who do not know restraint after achieving success will inevitably invite disaster. Countless heroes throughout history have fallen from their peaks due to arrogance, fulfilling the prophecy of the 'arrogant dragon.' This line teaches the highest wisdom: know when to advance and when to retreat, understand what to gain and what to release. The full moon must wane, the brimming cup must overflow—these are inviolable laws of nature.

judgmentZh: 坤：元，亨，利牝马之贞。君子有攸往，先迷后得主。利西南得朋，东北丧朋。安贞吉。
judgmentEn: The Receptive brings about sublime success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare.
imageZh: 地势坤，君子以厚德载物。
imageEn: The earth's condition is receptive devotion. Thus the superior man who has breadth of character carries the outer world.
interpretationZh: 坤卦象征地，代表柔顺、包容、承载。六爻皆阴，纯阴至柔，是最具承载力量的卦象。
interpretationEn: Kun represents earth and pure receptive power. All six lines are yin, symbolizing devotion, nurturing, and the capacity to sustain.

lines.textZh: 初六：履霜，坚冰至。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: When there is hoarfrost underfoot, solid ice is not far off.
lines.interpretationZh: 踩到薄霜，预示严冬将至，见微知著。
lines.interpretationEn: Frost underfoot signals that solid ice is coming. Heed early signs.
lines.detailZh: 初六为坤卦最下一爻，阴气初凝，如霜降大地。“履霜坚冰至”是一个深刻的因果警示：踩到薄霜时，就要意识到严冬即将来临。事物的发展都有征兆，小的变化积累到一定程度就会引发质变。这一爻教导我们要有远见和敏锐的洞察力——看到苗头就要警觉，防微杜渐，不要等到问题严重了才后悔。在现代生活中，这相当于注意身体的小信号、市场的微妙变化、关系中的细微裂痕。
lines.detailEn: The first six is the lowest line of Kun, where yin energy first condenses, like frost settling on the earth. 'When there is hoarfrost underfoot, solid ice is not far off' is a profound warning about cause and effect: when you step on thin frost, you must realize that deep winter is approaching. All developments show early signs—small changes accumulate until they trigger fundamental transformation. This line teaches foresight and keen observation: notice the first signs and take preventive action rather than waiting until problems become severe.

lines.textZh: 六二：直，方，大，不习无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Straight, square, great. Without purpose, yet nothing remains unfurthered.
lines.interpretationZh: 正直、方正、宏大，自然而然，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Upright, square, and great. Natural virtue brings success without effort.
lines.detailZh: 六二是坤卦的核心爻位，阴爻居阴位，得位得中，是坤德最完美的体现。“直方大，不习无不利”描述了大地最本质的品格：正直、方正、广大。这三个字看似简单，却是极高的修养境界——“直”是内心的正直不阿，“方”是行事的规矩方正，“大”是胸怀的宽广包容。达到这种境界的人，不需要刻意学习就能处处顺利，因为他的品德本身就是最好的通行证。
lines.detailEn: The second six is the core line of Kun—a yin line in a yin position, perfectly placed and centered, representing the fullest expression of Kun's virtue. 'Straight, square, great—without purpose, yet nothing remains unfurthered' describes the earth's essential character: upright, principled, and vast. These three qualities represent an extremely high level of cultivation—'straight' is inner integrity, 'square' is principled conduct, and 'great' is expansive tolerance. One who achieves this state needs no deliberate effort to succeed, for their character itself is the best passport.

lines.textZh: 六三：含章可贞，或从王事，无成有终。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Hidden lines. One is able to remain persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 内含文采而不显露，辅佐他人成事，虽无功名但有善终。
lines.interpretationEn: Conceal your talents. Serve others faithfully for a good outcome.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴爻居阳位，处于下卦之顶，位置不安。“含章可贞，或从王事，无成有终”教导一种内敛的智慧：有才华但不张扬（含章），保持正固（可贞），即使参与重大事务也不居功（无成有终）。这是坤卦“辅佐”精神的集中体现——真正的智者懂得隐藏锋芒，把功劳让给领导者，自己甘居幕后。在职场中，这种品质反而最容易获得长久的信任和重用。
lines.detailEn: The third six is a yin line in a yang position at the top of the lower trigram—an uneasy placement. 'Hidden lines, one may persevere. If following in service of a king, seek not works but bring to completion' teaches the wisdom of restraint: possessing talent without flaunting it, maintaining steadfastness, and not claiming credit even when participating in great undertakings. This is the concentrated expression of Kun's spirit of support—the truly wise know how to conceal their brilliance, yield credit to leaders, and willingly work behind the scenes.

lines.textZh: 六四：括囊，无咎无誉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: A tied-up sack. No blame, no praise.
lines.interpretationZh: 扎紧口袋，谨慎收敛，虽无赞誉但也无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: A tied sack. Caution brings neither blame nor praise.
lines.detailZh: 六四进入上卦，阴爻居阴位，处于谨慎收敛的阶段。“括囊，无咎无誉”的意象是把口袋扎紧——既不会有过失，也不会有赞誉。这是一种极端谨慎的处世态度，适用于危险或敏感的时期。当外部环境不利时，最明智的做法就是收敛锋芒、少说少做、保全自身。虽然不会获得荣耀，但也不会招来灾祸。在现代语境中，这相当于在公司动荡期保持低调，不站队、不表态，静观其变。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six enters the upper trigram—a yin line in a yin position, in a phase of cautious restraint. 'A tied-up sack—no blame, no praise' presents the image of tying a bag shut: neither fault nor glory will come. This represents an extremely cautious approach to life, suitable for dangerous or sensitive periods. When external conditions are unfavorable, the wisest course is to restrain oneself, say less, do less, and preserve one's position. Though no glory comes, neither does disaster. In modern terms, this is like keeping a low profile during corporate upheaval—not taking sides, not making declarations, watching and waiting.

lines.textZh: 六五：黄裳，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: A yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 黄色下裳，谦逊居中，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: Yellow garment symbolizes modesty and centrality. Supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五是坤卦的尊位，阴爻居阳位，以柔居刚，象征以谦逊的姿态处于高位。“黄裳，元吉”中“黄”为中央之色，代表中庸之道；“裳”为下衣，代表谦逊居下。身居高位却保持谦逊中庸，这是最吉祥的状态。历史上许多成功的辅政者都体现了这一爻的精神——如周公辅成王、诸葛亮辅刘禅，身居要职却从不僭越，以“黄裳”之德赢得千古美名。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six occupies Kun's honored position—a yin line in a yang place, softness dwelling in firmness, symbolizing humility in high position. In 'A yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune,' yellow represents the color of the center and the middle way, while the lower garment represents modesty and deference. Maintaining humility and moderation while in a position of power is the most auspicious state. Many successful regents throughout history embodied this line's spirit—like the Duke of Zhou assisting King Cheng, or Zhuge Liang serving Liu Shan, holding great power yet never overstepping bounds.

lines.textZh: 上六：龙战于野，其血玄黄。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Dragons fight in the meadow. Their blood is black and yellow.
lines.interpretationZh: 阴极生变，两龙相争，阴阳交战，有所损伤。
lines.interpretationEn: Yin reaches its extreme. Conflict arises with inevitable losses.
lines.detailZh: 上六是坤卦最高一爻，阴气已达极盛，与乾卦的“亢龙有悔”相呼应。“龙战于野，其血玄黄”描绘了阴阳激烈冲突的惨烈场景——阴气过盛到极点，必然与阳气发生正面冲突，两败俱伤。“玄黄”即天地之色（天玄地黄），象征天地秩序被打破。这一爻警示我们：任何力量发展到极端都会引发冲突。柔顺过度就变成了固执，包容过度就变成了纵容。保持阴阳平衡，才是长久之道。
lines.detailEn: The top six is the highest line of Kun, where yin energy has reached its absolute peak, echoing Qian's 'arrogant dragon will have cause to repent.' 'Dragons fight in the meadow. Their blood is black and yellow' depicts the devastating scene of extreme yin-yang conflict—when yin energy reaches its zenith, it inevitably clashes head-on with yang, resulting in mutual destruction. 'Black and yellow' are the colors of heaven and earth, symbolizing the shattering of cosmic order. This line warns that any force pushed to its extreme will provoke conflict. Excessive yielding becomes stubbornness; excessive tolerance becomes enabling. Maintaining yin-yang balance is the only lasting path.

overviewZh: 坤卦是《周易》六十四卦之第二卦，由六个阴爻组成，纯阴至柔，象征大地承载万物。坤为地，代表宇宙间最深厚、最包容的滋养力量。如果说乾卦是创造的源头，坤卦则是孕育的母体——万物依赖大地而生长，却从不见大地自夸其功。坤卦的核心精神是“厚德载物”——大地沉默而宽广，以无限的包容承载一切。坤卦强调顺势而为、柔中有刚，不是被动的服从，而是以柔克刚的大智慧。
overviewEn: Kun (The Receptive) is the second hexagram of the I Ching, composed of six broken yin lines representing pure receptive power. It symbolizes the earth and the nurturing force that sustains all life. If Qian is the source of creation, Kun is the womb of cultivation—all things depend on the earth to grow, yet the earth never boasts of its contributions. The core spirit of Kun is 'supporting all things with great virtue'—the earth is silent and vast, bearing everything with infinite tolerance. Kun emphasizes following the natural flow and finding strength within gentleness, not passive submission but the great wisdom of overcoming hardness with softness.
judgmentDetailZh: 坤卦卦辞“元亨，利牝马之贞。君子有攸往，先迷后得主，利。西南得朋，东北丧朋，安贞吉”蕴含深刻的处世哲学。“牝马”即母马，性情柔顺而善于奔走，象征坤卦柔顺而不失行动力的品质。“先迷后得主”告诫我们：坤道以辅佐为本，不宜争先，初时可能迷失方向，但只要坚守正道，终将找到值得追随的方向。“西南得朋”指同类相聚，“东北丧朋”指与异类分离——这是告诉我们要善于选择同道中人。《彖传》释曰：“至哉坤元，万物资生，乃顺承天”，坤的伟大在于它是万物生长的根基，顺应天道而成就一切。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Kun—'Supreme success, furthering through the perseverance of a mare. If the superior man undertakes something and tries to lead, he goes astray; but if he follows, he finds guidance'—contains profound philosophy. The mare symbolizes Kun's quality of being gentle yet capable of action. 'First confusion, then finding the master' teaches that the way of Kun is to support rather than lead; one may initially lose direction, but by holding to the right path, one will find worthy guidance. 'In the southwest, find friends; in the northeast, lose friends' advises choosing companions wisely. The Commentary states: 'Perfect indeed is the sublimity of the Receptive. All beings owe their birth to it, because it receives the heavenly with devotion.'
imageDetailZh: 象辞“地势坤，君子以厚德载物”与乾卦的“自强不息”并称为中华文明最核心的两大精神。“地势坤”描述大地的特质——低处而厚重，沉稳而广阔，默默承载山川河流、万物生灵。“厚德载物”则是从地道推及人道：有德行的人应当效法大地的精神，以宽厚的品德包容万事万物。这里的“厚德”不是软弱，而是一种深沉的力量——能容纳不同意见，能承受巨大压力，能在沉默中滋养万物。清华大学校训的后半句正是取自此处，与乾卦的“自强不息”共同构成刚柔并济的完整人格理想。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'The earth's condition is receptive devotion; thus the superior man who has breadth of character carries the outer world' stands alongside Qian's 'tireless self-strengthening' as the two most fundamental spiritual principles of Chinese civilization. 'The earth's condition' describes the nature of the ground—low yet substantial, steady yet vast, silently bearing mountains, rivers, and all living things. 'Carrying the outer world with breadth of character' extends this earthly principle to human conduct: a person of virtue should emulate the earth's spirit, embracing all things with generous virtue. This 'breadth of character' is not weakness but a profound strength—the capacity to accommodate different views, withstand great pressure, and nurture all things in silence.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】坤卦提示在职场中要善于辅佐和配合，不必事事争先。作为团队成员，发挥支撑作用往往比争当领导更有价值；作为管理者，要学会倾听和包容，以柔性管理凝聚团队。“先迷后得主”提醒我们：初入新环境时不要急于表现，先观察学习，找到正确的方向再发力。【感情】坤卦代表包容和滋养的能量，提示在关系中要学会倾听、理解和支持对方。不是一味迁就，而是以温柔的力量化解矛盾。“厚德载物”的精神用在感情中，就是用宽广的胸怀接纳对方的不完美。【财运】坤卦象征稳健和积累，适合长期投资、稳扎稳打，不宜冒进投机。像大地一样厚积薄发，财富自然水到渠成。【健康】坤卦对应脾胃和腹部，提醒注意消化系统保养，饮食规律，避免过度操劳。效法大地的沉稳，保持心态平和。
modernApplicationEn: 【Career】Kun advises excelling in support and collaboration rather than always competing for the lead. As a team member, playing a supporting role often creates more value than vying for leadership. 'First confusion, then finding guidance' reminds us not to rush to prove ourselves in new environments—observe, learn, then act.【Love】Kun represents nurturing energy, suggesting we listen, understand, and support our partners. Not blind accommodation, but resolving conflicts with gentle strength.【Wealth】Kun symbolizes stability and accumulation, favoring long-term investment and steady progress over speculation. Like the earth building up gradually, wealth comes naturally through patience.【Health】Kun corresponds to the spleen, stomach, and abdomen—maintain regular eating habits and avoid overwork. Emulate the earth's steadiness and keep a peaceful mindset.
historicalStoryZh: 坤卦最著名的典故与周文王有关。相传周文王被商纣王囚禁于羑里七年，正是在这段至暗时刻，他深悟坤卦“厚德载物”的道理——以大地般的忍耐承受苦难，韬光养晦，最终为周朝八百年基业奠定了根基。另一个典故是大禹治水。大禹效法坤卦“顺势而为”的智慧，不与洪水硬抗，而是疏导引流，顺应水性，终于平定水患。这正是坤卦“柔顺”精神的最佳体现——不是软弱的屈服，而是顺应自然规律的大智慧。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous story associated with Kun involves King Wen of Zhou. Imprisoned by the tyrant King Zhou of Shang for seven years at Youli, King Wen deeply understood Kun's principle of 'bearing all with great virtue'—enduring suffering with earth-like patience, biding his time, ultimately laying the foundation for the 800-year Zhou dynasty. Another classic story is Yu the Great's flood control. Rather than fighting the waters head-on, Yu followed Kun's wisdom of 'going with the flow,' channeling and redirecting the floods according to water's natural tendencies. This perfectly embodies Kun's spirit of yielding—not weak submission, but the great wisdom of working with natural laws.
relatedHexagramsNote: 坤卦的错卦为乾卦（第1卦），阴阳完全相反，刚柔互补；综卦亦为坤卦本身（上下颠倒不变）；互卦为坤卦（取二三四五爻组成的内卦仍为纯阴）。乾坤两卦互为表里，共同构成《周易》的根基，一刚一柔，一创一育。

judgmentZh: 屯：元，亨，利，贞，勿用有攸往，利建侯。
judgmentEn: Difficulty at the Beginning works supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Nothing should be undertaken. It furthers one to appoint helpers.
imageEn: Clouds and thunder: the image of Difficulty at the Beginning. Thus the superior man brings order out of confusion.
interpretationZh: 屯卦象征初生的艰难，如草木破土而出。万事开头难，但蕴含生机，宜坚守正道。
interpretationEn: Zhun symbolizes the difficulty of new beginnings, like a sprout breaking through earth. Persevere through initial hardship.

lines.textZh: 初九：磐桓，利居贞，利建侯。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Hesitation and hindrance. It furthers one to remain persevering. It furthers one to appoint helpers.
lines.interpretationZh: 徘徊不前，宜坚守正道，寻求帮助。
lines.interpretationEn: Hesitation at the start. Stay firm and seek capable helpers.
lines.detailZh: 初九为屯卦唯一的阳爻在下卦，阳刚居初位，如创业者迈出第一步。"磐桓"形容巨石般的阻碍使人徘徊不前，但这并非退缩的理由。"利居贞"告诫此时应当安守正道，不要急于冒进；"利建侯"则指出应当寻找帮手、建立根据地。初九虽然处境艰难，但作为阳爻具有刚健的本质，只要沉住气、打好基础、广结善缘，未来必有大展宏图之日。这正如创业初期，最重要的不是快速扩张，而是找准定位、组建核心团队。
lines.detailEn: The first nine is the only yang line in the lower trigram of Zhun, representing a strong force at the very beginning—like an entrepreneur taking the first step. 'Hesitation and hindrance' describes boulder-like obstacles causing one to pace back and forth, yet this is no reason to retreat. 'It furthers one to remain persevering' advises holding to the right path without rushing forward; 'it furthers one to appoint helpers' points to the solution of finding allies and establishing a base. Though the first nine faces difficult circumstances, as a yang line it possesses inherent strength. By staying calm, building solid foundations, and cultivating good relationships, great achievements will come in time. This mirrors the startup phase where the priority is not rapid expansion but finding the right positioning and assembling a core team.

lines.textZh: 六二：屯如邅如，乘马班如。匪寇婚媾，女子贞不字，十年乃字。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Difficulties pile up. Horse and wagon part. He is not a robber; he wants to woo.
lines.interpretationZh: 困难重重，但来者并非敌人而是求婚者，耐心等待终有好结果。
lines.interpretationEn: Difficulties mount but the stranger comes in peace. Patience brings union.
lines.detailZh: 六二阴爻居阴位，柔顺得正，却面临重重困难。"屯如邅如"形容进退维谷、踌躇不前的窘境；"乘马班如"描绘骑马欲行却不断回旋的焦虑。然而转折在于"匪寇婚媾"——看似来犯的敌人，实际上是来求婚的。这是一个关于误判的深刻教训：在困难时期，我们容易把善意当作恶意，把机会当作威胁。"女子贞不字，十年乃字"告诫要有耐心，守住操守，即使等待十年也终将迎来美好的结合。在现代语境中，这提醒我们在逆境中不要草率决策，要分辨真正的敌友。
lines.detailEn: The second six is a yin line in a yin position—gentle and correctly placed, yet facing layer upon layer of difficulty. 'Difficulties pile up' describes the predicament of being unable to advance or retreat; 'horse and wagon part' depicts the anxiety of trying to ride forward but constantly circling back. The turning point lies in 'he is not a robber; he wants to woo'—what appears to be an approaching enemy is actually a suitor. This offers a profound lesson about misjudgment: during difficult times, we easily mistake goodwill for hostility and opportunity for threat. 'The maiden remains faithful and does not pledge herself; after ten years she pledges herself' counsels patience and integrity—even if one must wait a decade, a beautiful union will eventually come. In modern terms, this reminds us not to make hasty decisions in adversity and to carefully distinguish true friends from foes.

lines.textZh: 六三：即鹿无虞，惟入于林中，君子几不如舍，往吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Whoever hunts deer without the forester only loses his way in the forest.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有向导就去追鹿，只会迷失在林中，不如放弃。
lines.interpretationEn: Hunting without a guide leads to getting lost. Better to let go.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴爻居阳位，不中不正，处境最为危险。"即鹿无虞"的意象极为生动：追逐鹿群却没有猎场管理员（虞人）引路，结果只会迷失在茫茫林海中。"君子几不如舍"是《周易》中最务实的忠告之一——与其盲目追逐不确定的目标，不如果断放弃。"往吝"警告强行前进只会招来困窘。这一爻的核心智慧是：没有充分准备和专业指导就贸然行动，不是勇敢而是鲁莽。在现代商业中，这相当于没有做市场调研就盲目投资，没有专业团队就仓促上马项目。
lines.detailEn: The third six is a yin line in a yang position—neither centered nor correct, in the most perilous situation. 'Hunting deer without the forester' presents a vivid image: pursuing game without a guide (the forester who knows the terrain), only to become hopelessly lost in the vast forest. 'The superior man would do better to give up the chase' is one of the most pragmatic pieces of advice in the entire I Ching—rather than blindly pursuing uncertain goals, it is better to decisively let go. 'Going leads to humiliation' warns that forcing ahead will only bring embarrassment. The core wisdom of this line is that acting without adequate preparation and professional guidance is not courage but recklessness. In modern business, this is equivalent to investing blindly without market research or launching projects hastily without a professional team.

lines.textZh: 六四：乘马班如，求婚媾，往吉，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Horse and wagon part. Strive for union. To go brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 虽有阻碍，但主动出击求合作，前往则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Despite obstacles, actively seek partnership. Going forward brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴爻居阴位，柔顺得正，虽处困境但态度正确。"乘马班如"再次出现骑马回旋的意象，说明仍有阻碍；但"求婚媾，往吉，无不利"表明此时主动出击是正确的选择。与六二不同，六四已经进入上卦，距离九五的领导者更近，条件更加成熟。这一爻教导我们：当时机相对成熟、方向已经明确时，就不应再犹豫不决，而要果断行动、主动寻求合作。放下身段、主动求助，往往能打开局面。在职场中，这相当于主动向上级或合作伙伴寻求支持。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six is a yin line in a yin position—gentle and correctly placed, facing difficulties but with the right attitude. 'Horse and wagon part' again presents the image of circling on horseback, indicating continued obstacles; yet 'strive for union, going brings good fortune, nothing unfavorable' shows that taking initiative is now the right choice. Unlike the second six, the fourth six has entered the upper trigram, closer to the leader at the fifth position, with more mature conditions. This line teaches that when timing is relatively ripe and direction is clear, one should not continue hesitating but act decisively and actively seek cooperation. Lowering one's pride and proactively seeking help often opens up new possibilities. In the workplace, this is equivalent to actively seeking support from superiors or partners.

lines.textZh: 九五：屯其膏，小贞吉，大贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Difficulties in blessing. A little perseverance brings good fortune. Great perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 恩泽难以施展，小事坚持则吉，大事强求则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: Blessings are blocked. Small perseverance succeeds; forcing big plans fails.
lines.detailZh: 九五阳爻居阳位，刚健中正，本应是最有利的位置，但在屯卦中却面临特殊困境。"屯其膏"意为恩泽（膏）被阻塞，难以施展。这是因为九五虽有能力和地位，但周围环境尚未理顺，政令难以畅通。"小贞吉，大贞凶"是极为精妙的辩证忠告：做小事、行小善可以逐步积累信任；但如果急于推行大政方针，反而会适得其反。这启示领导者在创业初期要循序渐进，先从小处着手建立威信，不可操之过急。治大国如烹小鲜，急火猛攻只会把事情搞砸。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is a yang line in a yang position—strong and centered, normally the most advantageous placement, yet in Zhun it faces a unique predicament. 'Difficulties in blessing' means that one's grace and bounty (gao) are blocked and cannot be distributed. Though the fifth nine possesses both ability and position, the surrounding environment has not yet been sorted out, and directives cannot flow smoothly. 'A little perseverance brings good fortune; great perseverance brings misfortune' offers exquisitely dialectical counsel: doing small things and small acts of kindness gradually builds trust, but rushing to implement grand policies will backfire. This teaches leaders that during the founding phase, they must proceed step by step, starting small to establish credibility rather than forcing the pace. Governing a great state is like cooking a small fish—too much heat and haste will ruin everything.

lines.textZh: 上六：乘马班如，泣血涟如。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Horse and wagon part. Bloody tears flow.
lines.interpretationZh: 进退两难，悲痛流泪，处境极为艰难。
lines.interpretationEn: Stuck in despair with tears of blood. An extremely difficult situation.
lines.detailZh: 上六是屯卦最高一爻，阴爻居阴位，处于困难的极点。"乘马班如"第三次出现，骑马回旋的意象已从犹豫变为绝望；"泣血涟如"更是描绘了血泪纵横的悲惨景象。这是屯卦中最凶险的一爻——困难已经积累到了极限，进退无路，只剩下悲伤和眼泪。然而从《周易》"物极必反"的哲学来看，极度的困难之后往往是转机的开始。上六的教训是：如果在创业过程中一味蛮干、不知变通，最终会陷入绝境。此时最需要的是深刻反省，寻找新的出路。
lines.detailEn: The top six is the highest line of Zhun—a yin line in a yin position, at the extreme point of difficulty. 'Horse and wagon part' appears for the third time, but the image of circling on horseback has transformed from hesitation into despair; 'bloody tears flow' depicts a scene of utter anguish. This is the most perilous line in Zhun—difficulties have accumulated to their limit, with no way forward or back, leaving only sorrow and tears. Yet from the I Ching's philosophy that 'things reverse at their extreme,' the depths of difficulty often mark the beginning of a turning point. The lesson of the top six is that if one blindly pushes forward during a venture without adapting, one will ultimately reach a dead end. What is most needed at this moment is deep reflection and the search for a new way out.

overviewZh: 屯卦是《周易》第三卦，由震下坎上组成，雷在水下，象征万物初生时的艰难困顿。"屯"字本义为草木萌芽破土之状，嫩芽顶着坚硬的土层奋力向上，充满生机却又步履维艰。屯卦是继乾坤之后的第一卦，《序卦传》云："有天地然后万物生焉。盈天地之间者唯万物，故受之以屯。屯者，盈也，屯者，物之始生也。"天地初开，万物草创，一切都在混沌中寻找秩序。屯卦的核心精神是：创业维艰，但生机蓬勃，只要坚守正道、善用人才，终能拨云见日。
overviewEn: Zhun (Difficulty at the Beginning) is the third hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Thunder (Zhen) below and Water (Kan) above. It depicts the primordial struggle of new life breaking through—like a tender sprout pushing against hard soil. As the first hexagram following Qian and Kun, Zhun represents the moment when heaven and earth have been established and the myriad things begin to emerge. The Sequence of Hexagrams commentary states: 'After heaven and earth come into existence, the myriad things are produced. What fills the space between heaven and earth are the myriad things.' Everything is in a state of creative chaos seeking order. The core teaching of Zhun is that beginnings are inherently difficult, yet they brim with vitality. By holding to the right path and enlisting capable helpers, one can navigate through the initial confusion to establish something enduring.
judgmentDetailZh: 屯卦卦辞"元亨利贞，勿用有攸往，利建侯"蕴含深刻的创业智慧。"元亨利贞"四德齐备，说明屯卦虽然艰难，但本质上是吉利的——困难只是暂时的，前途是光明的。"勿用有攸往"告诫在混乱初期不宜贸然行动，要先稳住阵脚。"利建侯"则指出破局之道：建立组织、分封诸侯、广纳人才。创业者不能单打独斗，必须组建团队、建立制度。《彖传》释曰："屯，刚柔始交而难生。动乎险中，大亨贞。"震为动、坎为险，在险难中行动，虽然艰辛，但只要方向正确、意志坚定，终将通达亨泰。这正是一切伟大事业开端的写照。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Zhun—'Supreme success, furthering through perseverance. Nothing should be undertaken. It furthers one to appoint helpers'—contains profound wisdom about new ventures. The presence of all four virtues (yuan, heng, li, zhen) indicates that despite its difficulties, Zhun is fundamentally auspicious—hardship is temporary, and the future is bright. 'Nothing should be undertaken' warns against rash action during initial chaos; one must first establish a stable footing. 'It furthers one to appoint helpers' reveals the key to breaking through: build organizations, delegate authority, and recruit talent. No founder can succeed alone; teams and systems must be established. The Commentary on the Decision explains: 'In Zhun, the firm and yielding first interact and difficulties arise. Movement in the midst of danger brings great success through perseverance.' Zhen represents movement and Kan represents danger—acting amid peril is arduous, but with correct direction and firm resolve, success will ultimately come.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"云雷屯，君子以经纶"是对治世之道的精辟概括。"云雷"描绘的是暴风雨来临前的景象——乌云密布、雷声隆隆，天地间充满了即将爆发的能量，但雨水尚未降下，万物仍在等待滋润。"经纶"本指整理丝线，引申为治理天下、经世济民。面对混乱的局面，君子不是消极等待，而是像理丝一样耐心梳理，建立秩序。经是纵线，纶是横线，经纬交织才能成布。这启示我们：在事业草创之际，最重要的不是急于求成，而是搭建框架、理顺关系、建立规则。正如织布必须先理好经纬线，创业也必须先做好顶层设计和基础建设。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'Clouds and thunder: the image of Difficulty at the Beginning. Thus the superior man brings order out of confusion' offers a masterful summary of governance in times of chaos. 'Clouds and thunder' depicts the scene before a great storm—dark clouds gather and thunder rumbles, the space between heaven and earth charged with energy about to burst forth, yet rain has not yet fallen and all things still await nourishment. 'Bringing order out of confusion' (jing lun) originally referred to sorting tangled silk threads, extended metaphorically to mean governing the world and serving the people. Facing chaotic circumstances, the superior man does not wait passively but patiently untangles the disorder like sorting silk, establishing order thread by thread. Jing are the vertical threads, lun the horizontal—only when warp and weft are properly interwoven can cloth be woven. This teaches us that at the founding of any enterprise, the priority is not rushing for results but building frameworks, clarifying relationships, and establishing rules.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】屯卦是典型的"创业卦"，提醒创业者万事开头难，不要期望一帆风顺。初期应专注于团队建设（"利建侯"），找到志同道合的合伙人，建立清晰的组织架构。不宜急于扩张（"勿用有攸往"），先把根基打牢。【感情】屯卦在感情中象征新关系的磨合期，双方都在适应和了解彼此。虽然会有误解和摩擦，但只要真心相待、耐心沟通，关系会逐渐稳固。"匪寇婚媾"提醒我们不要误判对方的善意。【财运】屯卦提示财运初期不宜冒进，适合小额试水、稳健积累。投资新项目要做好充分调研，不要被短期困难吓退，也不要盲目乐观。【健康】屯卦对应肾脏和泌尿系统（坎水），提醒注意水液代谢，避免过度疲劳，初期养生重在打好体质基础。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Zhun is the quintessential 'startup hexagram,' reminding entrepreneurs that all beginnings are difficult. The early phase should focus on team building ('it furthers one to appoint helpers'), finding like-minded partners, and establishing clear organizational structures. Avoid premature expansion ('nothing should be undertaken'); solidify the foundation first. In relationships, Zhun symbolizes the adjustment period of new connections—both parties are adapting and learning about each other. Though misunderstandings and friction will arise, sincere hearts and patient communication will gradually stabilize the bond. 'He is not a robber; he wants to woo' reminds us not to misjudge others' good intentions. For finances, Zhun advises against aggressive moves in the early stage; small-scale testing and steady accumulation are preferred. Research thoroughly before investing in new projects—neither be frightened by short-term difficulties nor blindly optimistic. For health, Zhun corresponds to the kidneys and urinary system (Kan water); pay attention to fluid metabolism, avoid excessive fatigue, and focus on building a strong physical foundation.
historicalStoryZh: 屯卦最著名的历史典故是周武王伐纣建周的故事。武王在牧野之战前面临重重困难——商朝势力庞大，诸侯人心未齐，天时地利皆不明朗。但武王效法屯卦"利建侯"的智慧，广封诸侯、团结盟友，最终一战功成，建立了延续八百年的周朝。另一个典故是刘邦创建汉朝。刘邦起兵之初不过是沛县一个小亭长，兵微将寡，屡战屡败。但他善于用人——萧何、张良、韩信各展所长，正是"利建侯"的最佳实践。从一介布衣到开国皇帝，刘邦的创业历程完美诠释了屯卦"万事开头难，坚持终有成"的精神。
historicalStoryEn: The most celebrated historical story associated with Zhun is King Wu of Zhou's campaign to overthrow the Shang dynasty. Before the decisive Battle of Muye, King Wu faced enormous difficulties—the Shang dynasty was powerful, the feudal lords were not yet united, and conditions were uncertain. But King Wu followed Zhun's wisdom of 'appointing helpers,' broadly enfeoffing lords and uniting allies, ultimately achieving victory in a single battle and establishing the Zhou dynasty that lasted eight hundred years. Another classic example is Liu Bang's founding of the Han dynasty. When Liu Bang first raised his banner, he was merely a minor local official from Pei County with few soldiers and fewer generals, suffering defeat after defeat. But he excelled at employing talent—Xiao He managed logistics, Zhang Liang provided strategy, and Han Xin commanded armies, each contributing their strengths. This was the finest practice of 'appointing helpers.' From commoner to founding emperor, Liu Bang's entrepreneurial journey perfectly embodies Zhun's spirit: all beginnings are difficult, but perseverance leads to success.
relatedHexagramsNote: 屯卦的错卦为鼎卦（第50卦），象征由草创到鼎盛的转化；综卦为蒙卦（第4卦），屯蒙相序，先有创生之难，后有启蒙之需；互卦为剥卦（第23卦），提醒创业过程中潜藏剥落消损的风险。

judgmentZh: 蒙：亨。匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我。初筮告，再三渎，渎则不告。利贞。
judgmentEn: Youthful Folly has success. It is not I who seek the young fool; the young fool seeks me. At the first oracle I inform him. If he asks two or three times, it is importunity. I give no information to the importunate. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 山下出泉，蒙；君子以果行育德。
imageEn: A spring wells up at the foot of the mountain: the image of Youth. Thus the superior man fosters his character by thoroughness in all that he does.
interpretationZh: 蒙卦象征蒙昧初开，如山下泉水初涌。启蒙教育需因势利导，学生主动求教方可施教。
interpretationEn: Meng symbolizes youthful ignorance, like a spring emerging from a mountain. Education requires the student to seek the teacher.

lines.textZh: 初六：发蒙，利用刑人，用说桎梏，以往吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: To make a fool develop, it furthers one to apply discipline.
lines.interpretationZh: 启发蒙昧，需要适当的纪律约束，但不可过度。
lines.interpretationEn: Discipline helps develop the young, but do not overdo it.
lines.detailZh: 初六为蒙卦最下一爻，阴爻居阳位，蒙昧最深。"发蒙，利用刑人，用说桎梏"看似严厉，实则蕴含教育的辩证法。"发蒙"是开启蒙昧，"利用刑人"指适当的惩戒有助于教育——就像给顽童立规矩，适度的纪律能帮助他们走上正轨。"用说桎梏"意为解除束缚，说明惩戒的目的不是惩罚本身，而是帮助蒙童摆脱无知的枷锁。"以往吝"则警告不可过度惩罚，否则适得其反。这一爻的智慧在于：教育需要规矩，但规矩是手段不是目的。
lines.detailEn: The first six is the lowest line of Meng—a yin line in a yang position, representing the deepest state of ignorance. 'To make a fool develop, it furthers one to apply discipline, to remove shackles' may seem harsh but contains the dialectics of education. 'Making a fool develop' means opening up ignorance; 'applying discipline' indicates that appropriate punishment aids education—like setting rules for an unruly child, moderate discipline helps them find the right path. 'Removing shackles' shows that the purpose of discipline is not punishment itself but helping the young break free from the chains of ignorance. 'Going leads to humiliation' warns against excessive punishment, which would be counterproductive. The wisdom of this line is that education requires rules, but rules are means, not ends.

lines.textZh: 九二：包蒙吉；纳妇吉；子克家。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: To bear with fools in kindliness brings good fortune. To know how to take women brings good fortune. The son is capable of taking charge of the household.
lines.interpretationZh: 包容蒙昧者则吉，以宽厚之心教导，如同治家有方。
lines.interpretationEn: Bearing with the foolish in kindness brings fortune. Lead with tolerance.
lines.detailZh: 九二是蒙卦的核心爻位，阳爻居阴位，刚柔相济，是理想的教育者形象。"包蒙吉"意为以宽厚包容的态度对待蒙昧者，这是教育的最高境界——不是居高临下地训斥，而是以慈悲心接纳学生的不足。"纳妇吉"比喻教育者要像迎娶新妇一样，以礼相待、以诚相感。"子克家"则说明好的教育能让后辈有能力持家立业。九二作为唯一的阳爻被五个阴爻环绕，象征一位优秀的老师被众多学生围绕，以一己之刚明照亮众人的蒙昧。
lines.detailEn: The second nine is the core line of Meng—a yang line in a yin position, blending firmness with gentleness, representing the ideal educator. 'To bear with fools in kindliness brings good fortune' means treating the ignorant with generous tolerance—the highest realm of education, not lecturing from above but accepting students' shortcomings with compassion. 'To know how to take women brings good fortune' metaphorically suggests the educator should treat students with courtesy and sincerity, like welcoming a bride. 'The son is capable of taking charge of the household' shows that good education enables the next generation to manage affairs independently. As the only yang line surrounded by five yin lines, the second nine symbolizes an excellent teacher surrounded by many students, illuminating their ignorance with singular clarity.

lines.textZh: 六三：勿用取女，见金夫，不有躬，无攸利。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Take not a maiden who loses her composure at the sight of a man of bronze.
lines.interpretationZh: 不要取没有定力的女子，见利忘义者不可信任。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not trust those who lose composure at the sight of wealth.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴爻居阳位，不中不正，是蒙卦中最不利的位置。"勿用取女，见金夫，不有躬，无攸利"描绘了一个见利忘义的形象：看到有钱有势的人就失去自持，丧失了自己的独立人格。这一爻警告的是教育中最大的失败——学生没有建立起独立的价值观，容易被外在的诱惑所左右。"金夫"象征物质诱惑，"不有躬"意为丧失自我。在现代语境中，这相当于一个人没有主见，随波逐流，看到什么流行就追什么，缺乏独立思考的能力。
lines.detailEn: The third six is a yin line in a yang position—neither centered nor correct, the most unfavorable placement in Meng. 'Take not a maiden who loses her composure at the sight of a man of bronze' depicts someone who abandons principles at the sight of wealth and power, losing their independent character. This line warns of the greatest failure in education—when students fail to establish independent values and are easily swayed by external temptations. 'The man of bronze' symbolizes material allure; 'losing composure' means losing oneself. In modern terms, this describes a person without convictions who follows every trend, chasing whatever is popular, lacking the capacity for independent thought.

lines.textZh: 六四：困蒙，吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Entangled folly brings humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 困于蒙昧之中，闭塞不通，有所困窘。
lines.interpretationEn: Trapped in ignorance leads to humiliation. Seek to break free.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴爻居阴位，远离九二的教育者，又被上下阴爻包围，陷入了"困蒙"的窘境。"困蒙，吝"描述的是被蒙昧所困、无法突破的状态——既没有好老师指导，周围也没有可以切磋的同学，独自困在无知的牢笼中。这是教育中最令人惋惜的情况：不是没有天赋，而是缺乏学习的环境和机会。在现代社会，这提醒我们教育资源的重要性——一个人如果长期处于信息闭塞、缺乏引导的环境中，即使有潜力也难以发挥。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six is a yin line in a yin position, far from the educator at the second nine and surrounded by other yin lines, trapped in 'entangled folly.' 'Entangled folly brings humiliation' describes being imprisoned by ignorance with no way to break through—without a good teacher for guidance and without peers for intellectual exchange, one is alone in the cage of unknowing. This is the most regrettable situation in education: not a lack of talent but a lack of learning environment and opportunity. In modern society, this reminds us of the critical importance of educational resources—if a person remains in an information-poor environment without guidance for too long, even great potential cannot be realized.

lines.textZh: 六五：童蒙，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Childlike folly brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 保持童真的求学之心，虚心受教则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Childlike openness to learning brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六五阴爻居阳位，以柔居尊，是蒙卦中最吉利的一爻。"童蒙，吉"简洁而深刻——保持童真般的求学之心，虚怀若谷地接受教育，这是最吉祥的状态。六五虽居尊位（君位），却以阴柔之姿虚心向九二的老师求教，体现了"不耻下问"的美德。历史上许多明君都具备这种品质：唐太宗虚心纳谏、康熙帝勤学不辍。这一爻告诉我们：无论地位多高、年龄多大，保持学习的谦逊和好奇心，永远是通向智慧的最佳途径。
lines.detailEn: The fifth six is a yin line in a yang position—softness in the place of honor, the most auspicious line in Meng. 'Childlike folly brings good fortune' is concise yet profound—maintaining a childlike eagerness to learn and accepting education with an open, humble heart is the most fortunate state. Though the fifth six occupies the sovereign's position, it humbly seeks instruction from the teacher at the second nine with gentle deference, embodying the virtue of 'not being ashamed to ask those below.' Many great rulers throughout history possessed this quality: Emperor Taizong of Tang humbly accepted remonstrance, and Emperor Kangxi studied tirelessly throughout his life. This line tells us that regardless of status or age, maintaining the humility and curiosity of a learner is always the best path to wisdom.

lines.textZh: 上九：击蒙；不利为寇，利御寇。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: In punishing folly, it does not further one to commit transgressions. The only thing that furthers is to prevent transgressions.
lines.interpretationZh: 惩戒蒙昧不可过度，防患于未然才是正道。
lines.interpretationEn: Punishing ignorance should not go too far. Prevention is better than punishment.
lines.detailZh: 上九是蒙卦最高一爻，阳爻居阴位，刚健处于极端。"击蒙；不利为寇，利御寇"描述了对待蒙昧的最后手段——"击"是强力打击、严厉惩戒。但关键在于后半句："不利为寇"意为不可以侵犯者的姿态去打击蒙昧，那样只会激起反抗；"利御寇"意为应以防御者的姿态来纠正错误，目的是保护而非伤害。这一爻的智慧在于区分"惩罚"与"教育"：惩罚是为了发泄怒气（为寇），教育是为了防止错误再犯（御寇）。在现代管理中，这提醒领导者批评下属时要对事不对人。
lines.detailEn: The top nine is the highest line of Meng—a yang line in a yin position, firmness at its extreme. 'In punishing folly, it does not further one to commit transgressions; the only thing that furthers is to prevent transgressions' describes the last resort in dealing with ignorance—'striking' represents forceful correction and severe discipline. But the crucial distinction lies in the second half: 'it does not further to act as an aggressor' means one must not attack ignorance with the posture of an invader, which would only provoke resistance; 'it furthers to defend against aggression' means correcting errors with a protective stance, aiming to shield rather than harm. The wisdom of this line lies in distinguishing punishment from education: punishment vents anger (acting as aggressor), while education prevents future mistakes (defending against aggression). In modern management, this reminds leaders to criticize the behavior, not the person.

overviewZh: 蒙卦是《周易》第四卦，由坎下艮上组成，山下有泉水涌出，象征蒙昧初开、启蒙教育。"蒙"字本义为蒙蔽、蒙昧，引申为童蒙未开的状态。《序卦传》云："屯者，物之始生也。物生必蒙，故受之以蒙。蒙者，蒙也，物之稚也。"万物初生之后必然处于蒙昧状态，需要教育和引导才能成长。蒙卦是中国最早的教育哲学，其核心思想是：教育的关键不在于老师主动灌输，而在于学生主动求学；启蒙的方式不是强制开化，而是因势利导、循序渐进。
overviewEn: Meng (Youthful Folly) is the fourth hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Water (Kan) below and Mountain (Gen) above—a spring welling up at the foot of a mountain, symbolizing the first stirrings of enlightenment from ignorance. The character 'meng' originally means covered or obscured, extended to describe the state of youthful inexperience. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'Zhun represents the beginning of things. When things are newly born, they are necessarily immature, hence Meng follows.' After the initial birth of all things comes an inevitable period of ignorance requiring education and guidance for growth. Meng represents China's earliest philosophy of education, with its core insight being that the key to education lies not in the teacher's active instruction but in the student's initiative to learn; enlightenment comes not through forced civilization but through guiding according to natural tendencies, step by step.
judgmentDetailZh: 蒙卦卦辞"亨。匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我。初筮告，再三渎，渎则不告。利贞"是中国教育思想的奠基之言。"匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我"确立了教育的根本原则——学生必须主动求教，老师才能有效施教。这与孔子"不愤不启，不悱不发"的教育理念一脉相承。"初筮告，再三渎，渎则不告"则强调了求学的态度：第一次虔诚请教，老师会认真解答；但如果反复追问同一个问题，那是对知识的亵渎，老师便不再回答。这不是老师傲慢，而是教育的智慧——只有学生真正思考过、困惑过，教育才能入心。"利贞"提醒教育必须坚守正道，不可投机取巧。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Meng—'Success. It is not I who seek the young fool; the young fool seeks me. At the first oracle I inform him. If he asks two or three times, it is importunity. I give no information to the importunate. Perseverance furthers'—is a foundational statement of Chinese educational thought. 'It is not I who seek the young fool; the young fool seeks me' establishes the fundamental principle of education: the student must actively seek instruction before the teacher can effectively teach. This aligns perfectly with Confucius's later principle of 'not enlightening those who are not eager, not inspiring those who are not struggling to express themselves.' 'At the first oracle I inform him; if he asks repeatedly, it is disrespectful' emphasizes the proper attitude toward learning: when one sincerely asks for the first time, the teacher answers earnestly; but repeatedly asking the same question shows disrespect for knowledge, and the teacher will no longer respond. This is not arrogance but educational wisdom—only when students have truly thought and struggled can teaching reach the heart.
imageDetailZh: 象辞"山下出泉，蒙；君子以果行育德"用山泉的意象完美诠释了启蒙教育的本质。山下涌出的泉水，清澈但尚不知流向何方——这正是蒙童的写照：天性纯净，但缺乏方向和经验。泉水从山脚流出，必须找到河道才能汇入大海；蒙童离开家庭，必须接受教育才能成才。"果行育德"是君子面对蒙昧时的两大修为："果行"是果断行动，该教则教、该罚则罚，不优柔寡断；"育德"是培育品德，教育的根本目的不是传授知识技能，而是涵养德行。这两者缺一不可——只有果行没有育德，教育沦为训练；只有育德没有果行，教育流于空谈。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'A spring wells up at the foot of the mountain: the image of Youth. Thus the superior man fosters his character by thoroughness in all that he does' uses the imagery of a mountain spring to perfectly illustrate the essence of education. The spring emerging at the mountain's base is clear but does not yet know where to flow—this is precisely the portrait of a young person: pure in nature but lacking direction and experience. The spring must find a riverbed to reach the ocean; the youth must receive education to reach maturity. 'Fostering character through thoroughness' encompasses two essential practices: 'thoroughness in action' means decisive conduct—teaching when teaching is needed, disciplining when discipline is called for, without hesitation; 'fostering character' means cultivating virtue, for the fundamental purpose of education is not transmitting knowledge and skills but nurturing moral character. Neither can be dispensed with—action without virtue reduces education to mere training; virtue without action reduces education to empty talk.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】蒙卦提醒职场新人要保持谦虚好学的态度，主动向前辈请教（"童蒙求我"），但不要反复问同样的问题（"再三渎"）。对于管理者和导师，蒙卦教导要因材施教、循序渐进，不要急于求成。培养新人需要耐心，但也要有原则和标准。【感情】蒙卦在感情中象征关系的懵懂期，双方对彼此的了解还很浅。此时需要真诚沟通、相互学习，不要凭想象判断对方。"包蒙吉"提醒我们要以包容之心对待伴侣的不成熟。【财运】蒙卦提示理财知识不足时不宜盲目投资，应先学习基础知识，向专业人士请教。"初筮告"意味着要抓住第一次学习的机会认真领悟。【健康】蒙卦对应肾水和脾胃（坎水艮土），提醒注意消化吸收功能，饮食宜清淡规律，如泉水般清净养身。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Meng reminds newcomers to maintain a humble, eager-to-learn attitude, proactively seeking guidance from seniors ('the young fool seeks me') without repeatedly asking the same questions ('importunity'). For managers and mentors, Meng teaches adapting instruction to individual needs and proceeding gradually without rushing results. Nurturing new talent requires patience but also principles and standards. In relationships, Meng symbolizes the naive early stage where both parties' understanding of each other is still shallow. Sincere communication and mutual learning are needed—do not judge your partner based on assumptions. 'To bear with fools in kindliness brings good fortune' reminds us to embrace a partner's immaturity with tolerance. For finances, Meng advises against blind investment when financial knowledge is insufficient; first learn the basics and consult professionals. 'At the first oracle I inform him' means seizing the first learning opportunity to truly understand. For health, Meng corresponds to kidney water and spleen-earth (Kan water, Gen earth); pay attention to digestive absorption, maintain a light and regular diet, and nourish the body like a clear spring.
historicalStoryZh: 蒙卦最著名的典故当属孔子与弟子的教学故事。孔子有教无类，但始终坚持"不愤不启，不悱不发"的原则——学生不到苦思冥想的地步不去开导，不到欲言又止的地步不去启发。这正是蒙卦"匪我求童蒙，童蒙求我"的生动实践。另一个典故是孟母三迁。孟子幼年时，孟母为了给儿子创造良好的学习环境，三次搬家——从墓地旁搬到市场边，再搬到学堂旁。孟母深谙蒙卦"果行育德"的道理：启蒙教育不仅需要言传身教，更需要营造适宜的成长环境。正是这种"因势利导"的教育智慧，成就了一代亚圣。
historicalStoryEn: The most famous historical story associated with Meng is Confucius's teaching philosophy with his disciples. Confucius taught without discrimination, yet always upheld the principle of 'not enlightening those who are not eager, not inspiring those who are not struggling to express themselves'—he would not guide students until they had thought deeply, nor prompt them until they were on the verge of articulation. This is the living practice of Meng's 'it is not I who seek the young fool; the young fool seeks me.' Another celebrated story is Mencius's mother moving three times. When Mencius was young, his mother relocated three times to create a proper learning environment—from beside a cemetery to near a marketplace, and finally next to a school. She deeply understood Meng's principle of 'fostering character through thoroughness': early education requires not only teaching by word and example but also creating a suitable environment for growth. It was precisely this wisdom of 'guiding according to natural tendencies' that produced one of China's greatest sages.
relatedHexagramsNote: 蒙卦的错卦为革卦（第49卦），蒙昧与变革相对，启蒙即是一种内在的革命；综卦为屯卦（第3卦），屯蒙相序，创生之后必有启蒙；互卦为复卦（第24卦），暗示教育的本质是回归天性、返璞归真。

judgmentZh: 需：有孚，光亨，贞吉。利涉大川。
judgmentEn: Waiting. If you are sincere, you have light and success. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 云上于天，需；君子以饮食宴乐。
imageEn: Clouds rise up to heaven: the image of Waiting. Thus the superior man eats and drinks, is joyous and of good cheer.
interpretationZh: 需卦象征等待，云气上升于天尚未成雨。有诚信则前途光明，宜耐心等待时机。
interpretationEn: Xu symbolizes waiting. Clouds rise but rain has not yet come. Sincerity and patience lead to success.

lines.textZh: 初九：需于郊，利用恒，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Waiting in the meadow. It furthers one to abide in what endures. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在郊外等待，保持恒心，不会有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting in the open. Maintain constancy and there will be no blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九阳爻居阳位，刚健得正，处于需卦最下方，距离上方的坎险最远。"需于郊"意为在郊外等待——远离危险，环境安全。"利用恒，无咎"告诫此时应保持恒心和日常节奏，不要因为远处的危险而自乱阵脚。郊外是城市的外围，象征危险尚远、局势尚可控。这一爻教导我们：当危机还在远方时，最好的应对是保持正常的生活和工作节奏，稳扎稳打地做好准备。过早的恐慌只会消耗精力，真正的智者在危机远未到来时从容不迫。
lines.detailEn: The first nine is a yang line in a yang position—strong and correctly placed, at the very bottom of Xu, farthest from the Kan danger above. 'Waiting in the meadow' means waiting in the open countryside—far from danger, in a safe environment. 'It furthers one to abide in what endures; no blame' advises maintaining constancy and daily rhythm without being thrown into disarray by distant danger. The meadow is the city's outskirts, symbolizing that danger is still far off and the situation remains manageable. This line teaches that when crisis is still distant, the best response is maintaining normal life and work rhythms while steadily preparing. Premature panic only wastes energy; the truly wise remain composed when crisis has not yet arrived.

lines.textZh: 九二：需于沙，小有言，终吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Waiting on the sand. There is some gossip. The end brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在沙滩上等待，虽有小口舌，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting on the sand. Minor talk arises but the outcome is fortunate.
lines.detailZh: 九二阳爻居阴位，刚柔相济，距离坎险更近了一步。"需于沙"意为在沙滩上等待——沙滩靠近水边，危险已经隐约可见。"小有言"指出现了一些闲言碎语和小摩擦，但"终吉"说明最终结果是好的。沙地不如郊外安稳，脚下已经有些松软，但尚未陷入泥沼。这一爻描绘的是危机逐渐逼近时的状态：周围开始出现一些不和谐的声音，压力在增大，但只要保持宽容大度、不与人计较，就能安然度过。在职场中，这相当于项目推进中遇到的小阻力和同事间的小摩擦。
lines.detailEn: The second nine is a yang line in a yin position—blending firmness with flexibility, one step closer to the Kan danger above. 'Waiting on the sand' means waiting at the beach—near the water's edge where danger is faintly visible. 'There is some gossip' indicates minor friction and idle talk, but 'the end brings good fortune' confirms the final outcome is positive. Sandy ground is less stable than the meadow—the footing is somewhat soft but one has not yet sunk into mud. This line depicts the state when crisis gradually approaches: discordant voices begin to appear around you, pressure increases, but as long as you maintain generosity and do not quarrel over trifles, you will pass through safely. In the workplace, this corresponds to minor resistance during project advancement and small frictions among colleagues.

lines.textZh: 九三：需于泥，致寇至。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Waiting in the mud brings about the arrival of the enemy.
lines.interpretationZh: 在泥泞中等待，招致敌人到来，处境危险。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting in the mud invites danger. The enemy approaches.
lines.detailZh: 九三阳爻居阳位，处于下卦之顶，紧邻上卦坎险，处境最为危急。"需于泥"意为在泥泞中等待——已经陷入了困境，行动受限。"致寇至"更是警告：在泥泞中挣扎会招来敌人的攻击。泥地是郊外、沙滩之后的第三个阶段，危险已经近在咫尺。这一爻的教训极为深刻：当你已经身处险境边缘时，任何轻率的举动都可能招致灾祸。此时最需要的是极度的谨慎和自制力——不要因为焦虑而做出冲动的决定，也不要因为恐惧而暴露自己的弱点。
lines.detailEn: The third nine is a yang line in a yang position at the top of the lower trigram, directly adjacent to the Kan danger of the upper trigram—the most critical situation. 'Waiting in the mud' means waiting while mired in difficulty—already trapped, with movement restricted. 'The arrival of the enemy' further warns that struggling in the mud will attract attack. Mud is the third stage after meadow and sand—danger is now at arm's length. The lesson of this line is extremely profound: when you are already at the edge of peril, any rash move could invite disaster. What is most needed is extreme caution and self-control—do not make impulsive decisions out of anxiety, and do not expose your weaknesses out of fear.

lines.textZh: 六四：需于血，出自穴。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: Waiting in blood. Get out of the pit.
lines.interpretationZh: 身处血泊之中，需从险境中脱身。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting amid blood. Escape from the dangerous pit.
lines.detailZh: 六四阴爻居阴位，已经进入坎险之中。"需于血，出自穴"是需卦中最惊心动魄的一爻——"血"象征伤害和危险，"穴"象征困境和陷阱。身处血泊之中，唯一的出路是"出自穴"——从洞穴中脱身。六四以柔居柔，虽然身处险境，但因为柔顺得正，能够顺应形势、随机应变，最终脱离危险。这一爻教导我们：当危机真正降临时，不要硬扛，要懂得灵活应变、寻找出路。有时候"退一步"不是懦弱，而是生存的智慧。在商业中，这相当于及时止损、战略撤退。
lines.detailEn: The fourth six is a yin line in a yin position, already within the Kan danger zone. 'Waiting in blood, getting out of the pit' is the most harrowing line in Xu—'blood' symbolizes harm and danger, 'pit' symbolizes entrapment. Amid bloodshed, the only way out is to 'get out of the pit'—escape from the cave. The fourth six, soft in a soft position, though situated in peril, can adapt to circumstances and respond flexibly because of its correct yielding nature, ultimately escaping danger. This line teaches that when crisis truly arrives, do not resist stubbornly but know how to adapt flexibly and find a way out. Sometimes 'stepping back' is not cowardice but the wisdom of survival. In business, this is equivalent to cutting losses in time and making a strategic retreat.

lines.textZh: 九五：需于酒食，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Waiting at meat and drink. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在酒食宴席中等待，安享其成，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Waiting with food and drink. Enjoy and persevere for good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九五阳爻居阳位，刚健中正，是需卦的核心爻位。"需于酒食，贞吉"回应了象辞"饮食宴乐"的主题——在等待的过程中，以酒食自娱，保持身心愉悦。九五虽处坎险之中，但因为刚健中正，能够在危险中保持从容。"酒食"不仅是物质的滋养，更是精神的安定。这一爻体现了需卦最高的智慧：真正的强者不是不遇到危险，而是在危险中依然能保持优雅和从容。"贞吉"提醒我们：享乐要有度，坚守正道才能最终获吉。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is a yang line in a yang position—strong and centered, the core line of Xu. 'Waiting at meat and drink; perseverance brings good fortune' echoes the Image text's theme of 'eating, drinking, and being joyous'—during the waiting period, one entertains oneself with food and drink, maintaining physical and mental well-being. Though the fifth nine is within the Kan danger zone, its strength and centrality allow it to maintain composure amid peril. 'Meat and drink' represent not only physical nourishment but also spiritual equanimity. This line embodies Xu's highest wisdom: the truly strong are not those who never encounter danger but those who maintain grace and composure within it. 'Perseverance brings good fortune' reminds us that enjoyment must have limits—only by holding to the right path can one ultimately achieve good fortune.

lines.textZh: 上六：入于穴，有不速之客三人来，敬之终吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One falls into the pit. Three uninvited guests arrive. Honor them, and in the end there will be good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 落入洞穴，有三位不速之客到来，以礼相待则终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Falling into the pit. Three unexpected guests arrive. Treat them with respect for good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 上六阴爻居阴位，处于需卦最高处，等待已经到了尽头。"入于穴"意为落入洞穴，处境看似绝望；但"有不速之客三人来"带来了转机——三位不请自来的客人出现了。"敬之终吉"告诫我们：面对意外的来访者，以礼相待就能化险为夷。这三位"不速之客"对应下卦乾的三个阳爻，象征刚健的力量前来救援。这一爻的深意在于：当等待到了极限、似乎走投无路时，往往会出现意想不到的转机。关键是保持开放和尊重的态度，不要因为困境而拒绝帮助。
lines.detailEn: The top six is a yin line in a yin position at the very peak of Xu—the waiting has reached its end. 'Falling into the pit' suggests a seemingly hopeless situation; yet 'three uninvited guests arrive' brings an unexpected turning point—three visitors appear without invitation. 'Honor them, and in the end there will be good fortune' advises that treating these unexpected visitors with respect will transform danger into safety. The 'three uninvited guests' correspond to the three yang lines of the lower Qian trigram, symbolizing strong forces coming to the rescue. The deeper meaning of this line is that when waiting reaches its limit and all seems lost, unexpected turning points often appear. The key is maintaining an open and respectful attitude—do not refuse help because of your predicament.

overviewZh: 需卦是《周易》第五卦，由乾下坎上组成，天上有云，云气蒸腾尚未化雨，象征等待的智慧。"需"字本义为等待、需要，引申为蓄势待发。《序卦传》云："物稚不可不养也，故受之以需。需者，饮食之道也。"万物幼小时需要滋养，而滋养需要等待时机。需卦的核心精神是：面对前方的险阻（坎水），不是鲁莽冲锋，而是以刚健之德（乾天）耐心等待，在等待中保持信心和从容。需卦教导我们，等待不是消极被动，而是一种积极的战略选择——养精蓄锐，以待天时。
overviewEn: Xu (Waiting) is the fifth hexagram of the I Ching, composed of Heaven (Qian) below and Water (Kan) above—clouds rising in the sky, vapor gathering but not yet falling as rain, symbolizing the wisdom of waiting. The character 'xu' originally means to wait or to need, extended to mean gathering strength before action. The Sequence of Hexagrams states: 'Young things cannot be left without nourishment, hence Xu follows. Xu is the way of food and drink.' All young things need nourishment, and nourishment requires waiting for the right moment. The core spirit of Xu is that when facing danger ahead (Kan water), one should not charge recklessly but wait patiently with strong virtue (Qian heaven), maintaining confidence and composure throughout. Xu teaches that waiting is not passive resignation but an active strategic choice—conserving energy and building strength while awaiting the right time.
judgmentDetailZh: 需卦卦辞"有孚，光亨，贞吉。利涉大川"揭示了等待的正确姿态。"有孚"即有诚信，这是等待的前提——内心必须有坚定的信念，相信等待是值得的。"光亨"意为光明通达，说明有诚信的等待必将迎来光明的前景。"贞吉"强调坚守正道才能获得吉祥。"利涉大川"则表明：经过充分的等待和准备之后，即使面对大江大河般的险阻也能顺利渡过。《彖传》释曰："需，须也。险在前也，刚健而不陷，其义不困穷矣。"前方虽有险阻，但因为内在刚健（乾德），所以不会陷入困境。这正是"以逸待劳"的战略智慧。
judgmentDetailEn: The judgment of Xu—'If you are sincere, you have light and success. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water'—reveals the proper posture of waiting. 'Sincerity' is the prerequisite for waiting—one must hold firm inner conviction that the wait is worthwhile. 'Light and success' means that sincere waiting will inevitably lead to a bright future. 'Perseverance brings good fortune' emphasizes that only by holding to the right path can one achieve auspicious outcomes. 'It furthers one to cross the great water' indicates that after sufficient waiting and preparation, even obstacles as formidable as great rivers can be successfully crossed. The Commentary explains: 'Xu means waiting. Danger lies ahead, but being strong and firm, one does not fall in—the meaning is that one will not be reduced to extremity.' Though danger lies ahead, inner strength (the virtue of Qian) prevents one from being trapped. This is the strategic wisdom of 'waiting at ease for the exhausted enemy.'
imageDetailZh: 象辞"云上于天，需；君子以饮食宴乐"是《周易》中最出人意料的象辞之一。面对前方的险阻，君子的应对方式竟然是"饮食宴乐"——吃喝享乐？这看似矛盾，实则蕴含深刻的智慧。"云上于天"描绘云气已经升到天上，雨水即将降临但尚未降下，这是一个需要等待的时刻。在等待的过程中，最明智的做法不是焦虑不安，而是保持身心的愉悦和充沛。"饮食"是滋养身体，"宴乐"是调养精神。真正的强者在危机来临之前，不是惶惶不可终日，而是从容不迫地养精蓄锐。这种"泰山崩于前而色不变"的气度，正是需卦所推崇的等待之道。
imageDetailEn: The Image text 'Clouds rise up to heaven: the image of Waiting. Thus the superior man eats and drinks, is joyous and of good cheer' is one of the most surprising Image texts in the entire I Ching. Facing danger ahead, the superior man's response is to eat, drink, and be merry? This seems contradictory but contains profound wisdom. 'Clouds rise up to heaven' depicts vapor that has ascended to the sky—rain is imminent but has not yet fallen, a moment requiring patience. During this waiting period, the wisest course is not anxious fretting but maintaining physical and mental vitality. 'Eating and drinking' nourishes the body; 'being joyous' nurtures the spirit. A truly strong person, before a crisis arrives, does not spend days in panic but calmly conserves energy and builds strength. This composure—remaining unmoved even if Mount Tai crumbles before one's eyes—is precisely the way of waiting that Xu advocates.
modernApplicationZh: 【事业】需卦是"等待的艺术"，提醒我们在时机未到时不要急于行动。求职者在等待offer时应继续提升自己；创业者在市场尚未成熟时应耐心打磨产品。"有孚光亨"告诉我们：只要方向正确、准备充分，等待本身就是一种积极的策略。【感情】需卦在感情中象征暧昧期或异地恋的等待。此时最重要的是保持信心和真诚（"有孚"），不要因为等待而焦虑或做出冲动的决定。好的感情值得等待。【财运】需卦提示投资要有耐心，不要追涨杀跌。"云上于天"象征利好消息即将到来但尚未兑现，此时应持仓等待而非频繁操作。长线投资者最能体会需卦的智慧。【健康】需卦对应消化系统和水液代谢，"饮食宴乐"直接提示要注意饮食调养，保持心情愉悦，不要因压力而暴饮暴食或食欲不振。
modernApplicationEn: In career, Xu is 'the art of waiting,' reminding us not to rush when the timing is not right. Job seekers waiting for offers should continue improving themselves; entrepreneurs should patiently refine their products when the market is not yet mature. 'Sincerity brings light and success' tells us that as long as the direction is correct and preparation is thorough, waiting itself is an active strategy. In relationships, Xu symbolizes the waiting of ambiguous phases or long-distance love. The most important thing is maintaining confidence and sincerity ('having faith') without letting anxiety drive impulsive decisions. Good love is worth waiting for. For finances, Xu advises patience in investing—do not chase rallies or panic-sell in drops. 'Clouds rise to heaven' symbolizes good news approaching but not yet materialized; hold positions and wait rather than trading frequently. Long-term investors best appreciate Xu's wisdom. For health, Xu corresponds to the digestive system and fluid metabolism; 'eating, drinking, and being joyous' directly advises paying attention to dietary nourishment, maintaining a cheerful mood, and avoiding stress-driven overeating or loss of appetite.
historicalStoryZh: 需卦最经典的历史典故是姜太公渭水垂钓。姜子牙年过七旬仍在渭水之滨直钩钓鱼，看似悠闲实则是在等待明主。他深知自己的才华终将被发现，以"饮食宴乐"的从容心态度过了漫长的等待期。最终周文王慧眼识珠，拜他为师，辅佐武王伐纣成功。另一个典故是诸葛亮隆中对策前的等待。诸葛亮隐居隆中十年，躬耕陇亩、博览群书，看似无所事事，实则在为出山做最充分的准备。他的"饮食宴乐"是读书交友、观察天下大势。当刘备三顾茅庐时，诸葛亮已经胸有成竹，一篇《隆中对》便定下了三分天下的战略蓝图。
historicalStoryEn: The most classic historical story associated with Xu is Jiang Taigong fishing at the Wei River. Past seventy years old, Jiang Ziya sat by the Wei River fishing with a straight hook—seemingly leisurely but actually waiting for an enlightened ruler. He knew his talents would eventually be recognized and spent the long waiting period with the composure of 'eating, drinking, and being of good cheer.' Eventually King Wen of Zhou recognized his worth, appointed him as advisor, and together they laid the groundwork for King Wu's successful overthrow of the Shang dynasty. Another story is Zhuge Liang's decade of waiting before his Longzhong Strategy. Zhuge Liang lived in seclusion at Longzhong for ten years, farming and reading extensively—appearing idle but actually making the most thorough preparations for his eventual emergence. His 'eating and being joyous' consisted of reading, making friends, and observing the great trends of the world. When Liu Bei visited three times, Zhuge Liang was fully prepared, and a single Longzhong Strategy laid out the blueprint for dividing the realm into three kingdoms.
relatedHexagramsNote: 需卦的错卦为晋卦（第35卦），等待与进取相对，需要等待时机成熟才能晋升；综卦为讼卦（第6卦），需讼相序，等待不成则生争讼；互卦为睽卦（第38卦），提醒等待过程中可能出现的分歧和误解。

judgmentZh: 讼：有孚，窒惕，中吉，终凶。利见大人，不利涉大川。
judgmentEn: Conflict. You are sincere and are being obstructed. A cautious halt halfway brings good fortune. Going through to the end brings misfortune. It furthers one to see the great man. It does not further one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 天与水违行，讼；君子以作事谋始。
imageEn: Heaven and water go their opposite ways: the image of Conflict. Thus in all his transactions the superior man carefully considers the beginning.
interpretationZh: 讼卦象征争讼，天水相违背。有诚信但受阻，中途停止则吉，执意到底则凶。
interpretationEn: Song symbolizes conflict. Heaven and water move in opposite directions. Stop halfway for fortune; pushing to the end brings misfortune.

lines.textZh: 初六：不永所事，小有言，终吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If one does not perpetuate the affair, there is a little gossip. In the end, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 不要把争讼之事持续下去，虽有小口舌，最终吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Do not prolong the conflict. Despite minor gossip, the end is fortunate.
lines.detailZh: 初六阴爻居阳位，处于讼卦最下方，争讼刚刚开始。"不永所事，小有言，终吉"给出了最明智的建议：不要把争讼之事持续下去。"不永"二字是关键——争端一旦开始，越早结束越好。"小有言"说明虽然会有一些闲言碎语和小摩擦，但只要及时止损，最终结果是吉利的。初六以阴柔居下，力量薄弱，本就不适合争讼。这一爻教导我们：当你处于弱势地位时，与其硬碰硬，不如尽早退出争端，保全自身。小不忍则乱大谋，忍一时之气可免长久之祸。
lines.detailEn: The first six is a yin line in a yang position at the very bottom of Song—the conflict has just begun. 'If one does not perpetuate the affair, there is a little gossip; in the end, good fortune comes' offers the wisest counsel: do not let the dispute continue. 'Not perpetuating' is the key—once a dispute begins, the sooner it ends, the better. 'A little gossip' indicates some idle talk and minor friction, but as long as losses are cut in time, the final outcome is fortunate. The first six, being yin and weak in the lowest position, is inherently unsuited for conflict. This line teaches that when you are in a weak position, rather than confronting strength head-on, it is better to exit the dispute early and preserve yourself. Failing to endure small provocations ruins great plans; bearing momentary anger prevents lasting disaster.

lines.textZh: 九二：不克讼，归而逋，其邑人三百户，无眚。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One cannot engage in conflict. One returns home, gives way. The people of his town, three hundred households, remain free of guilt.
lines.interpretationZh: 争讼不胜，退回家乡避让，可保一方平安。
lines.interpretationEn: Unable to win the conflict, retreat home. This protects your community.
lines.detailZh: 九二阳爻居阴位，刚健但不得位，在争讼中处于不利地位。"不克讼"意为无法在争讼中获胜；"归而逋"是回到家乡躲避。"其邑人三百户，无眚"说明虽然自己退让了，但保全了整个社区的安宁。九二虽有实力（阳爻），但面对上方九五的强大对手，明智地选择了退让。这一爻的智慧在于：争讼不是只关乎个人的胜负，还牵涉到身边人的安危。有时候个人的退让能换来集体的平安。在现代语境中，这相当于企业家为了保全团队而选择庭外和解。
lines.detailEn: The second nine is a yang line in a yin position—strong but not in its proper place, at a disadvantage in the conflict. 'One cannot engage in conflict' means being unable to win the dispute; 'returning home and retreating' means going back to one's hometown to avoid further confrontation. 'The people of his town, three hundred households, remain free of guilt' shows that although one personally retreated, the entire community's peace was preserved. Though the second nine has strength (being a yang line), facing the powerful opponent at the fifth nine above, it wisely chooses to yield. The wisdom of this line is that conflict is not merely about personal victory or defeat—it also involves the safety of those around you. Sometimes personal concession buys collective peace. In modern terms, this is like an entrepreneur choosing an out-of-court settlement to protect the team.

lines.textZh: 六三：食旧德，贞厉，终吉。或从王事，无成。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: To nourish oneself on ancient virtue induces perseverance. Danger. In the end, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 依靠旧有的德行生活，虽有危险但终获吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Live on past virtues. Danger exists but the end brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 六三阴爻居阳位，不中不正，处境危险。"食旧德，贞厉，终吉"教导在争讼的漩涡中要依靠过去积累的德行和声誉来自保。"食旧德"意为以旧有的功德为生——不去争夺新的利益，安守本分。"贞厉"说明即使坚守正道也会有危险，但"终吉"保证最终结果是好的。"或从王事，无成"则提醒：即使参与了重大事务，也不要居功。六三的智慧在于：在纷争的环境中，低调做人、不争不抢，靠过去的积累和口碑度过难关，比卷入新的争端要安全得多。
lines.detailEn: The third six is a yin line in a yang position—neither centered nor correct, in a dangerous situation. 'To nourish oneself on ancient virtue induces perseverance; danger, but in the end good fortune' teaches that amid the whirlpool of conflict, one should rely on previously accumulated virtue and reputation for protection. 'Nourishing on ancient virtue' means living on past merits—not grasping for new gains but keeping to one's place. 'Perseverance brings danger' indicates that even holding to the right path involves risk, but 'in the end good fortune' guarantees a positive outcome. 'If following in service of a king, seek not works' further advises against claiming credit even when participating in important affairs. The wisdom of the third six is that in contentious environments, maintaining a low profile, not competing or grabbing, and relying on past accumulation and reputation to weather difficulties is far safer than becoming entangled in new disputes.

lines.textZh: 九四：不克讼，复即命，渝，安贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: One cannot engage in conflict. One turns back and submits to fate, changes one's attitude, and finds peace in perseverance. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 争讼不胜，回归正命，改变态度，安守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Cannot win the conflict. Return to fate, change attitude, find peace.
lines.detailZh: 九四阳爻居阴位，刚而不正，本有争讼之心。"不克讼，复即命，渝，安贞吉"描述了一个深刻的转变过程：争讼不能取胜（"不克讼"），于是回归天命（"复即命"），改变了原来的态度（"渝"），最终在安守正道中获得吉祥（"安贞吉"）。这一爻最可贵的是"渝"字——改变。九四原本刚强好争，但在争讼失败后能够反省自身、改变态度，从争强好胜转为安分守己。这种知错能改的品质，比从不犯错更加难得。在现代生活中，这提醒我们：输了官司不可怕，可怕的是输了还不反省。
lines.detailEn: The fourth nine is a yang line in a yin position—strong but not correctly placed, originally inclined toward conflict. 'One cannot engage in conflict; one turns back and submits to fate, changes one's attitude, and finds peace in perseverance—good fortune' describes a profound transformation: unable to win the dispute ('cannot engage'), one returns to accept destiny ('submits to fate'), changes the original attitude ('changes'), and ultimately finds good fortune in peaceful adherence to the right path ('peace in perseverance'). The most valuable element of this line is the word 'change.' The fourth nine was originally strong and combative, but after failing in conflict, it reflects on itself and transforms its attitude from competitive aggression to contented acceptance. This quality of being able to recognize and correct one's mistakes is rarer than never making mistakes at all. In modern life, this reminds us that losing a lawsuit is not frightening—what is frightening is losing and still not reflecting.

lines.textZh: 九五：讼，元吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: To contend before him brings supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 在公正的裁判面前争讼，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: Contending before a just judge brings supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九五阳爻居阳位，刚健中正，是讼卦中最吉利的一爻。"讼，元吉"简洁有力——在公正的裁判面前争讼，大吉大利。九五居尊位，象征公正无私的裁判者或仲裁者。当争讼双方都有道理时，需要一个德高望重、公正无私的权威来做出裁决。九五正是这样的角色——不偏不倚、明察秋毫。这一爻告诉我们：争讼本身不一定是坏事，关键在于是否有公正的裁决机制。在法治社会中，法院就是"九五"的角色，公正的司法是化解争端的最终保障。
lines.detailEn: The fifth nine is a yang line in a yang position—strong and centered, the most auspicious line in Song. 'To contend before him brings supreme good fortune' is concise and powerful—presenting one's case before a just judge brings the greatest fortune. The fifth nine occupies the sovereign's position, symbolizing an impartial and fair arbiter or mediator. When both parties in a dispute have legitimate claims, a respected, impartial authority is needed to render judgment. The fifth nine is precisely this figure—unbiased and perceptive. This line tells us that conflict itself is not necessarily bad; what matters is whether a fair adjudication mechanism exists. In a society governed by law, the court plays the role of the 'fifth nine,' and just judiciary is the ultimate guarantee for resolving disputes.

lines.textZh: 上九：或锡之鞶带，终朝三褫之。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Even if by chance a leather belt is bestowed on one, by the end of a morning it will have been snatched away three times.
lines.interpretationZh: 即使侥幸获赏，也会被反复夺去，争讼到底终无好结果。
lines.interpretationEn: Even if rewarded, it will be taken away repeatedly. Conflict to the end fails.
lines.detailZh: 上九阳爻居阴位，处于讼卦最高处，争讼已到极端。"或锡之鞶带，终朝三褫之"描绘了一个讽刺的场景：即使侥幸在争讼中获胜，得到了赏赐的皮带（鞶带），但在一个早晨之内就被夺走三次。这说明靠争讼获得的利益是不稳固的，随时可能失去。上九把争讼进行到底，虽然暂时赢了，但树敌太多、消耗太大，得到的东西根本守不住。这是讼卦"终凶"的最终验证——争到最后，即使赢了也是输了。在现代社会，这警示那些热衷于打官司的人：赢了诉讼却输了人心，得不偿失。
